title: Advanced Non-Cartesian MR Fingerprinting Reconstruction with Improved GROG Framework
institution: The Hong Kong Polytechnic University, Dept. of Health Technology and Informatics
supervisors: Prof. Tian Li & Dr. Chenyang Liu
summary: Accelerated non-Cartesian spiral MRF reconstruction via reengineered self-calibrating GROG for precise spiral-to-Cartesian gridding.
bullets: Resolved the fundamental speed bottleneck of non-Cartesian MRF to enable fast, high-fidelity quantitative imaging.

title: Large-Scale MRA Imaging Database and Generalist AI Foundation Model for Cerebrovascular Stenosis/Occlusion
institution: Peking University, Institute of Medical Technology
summary: Leading an anatomy-aware AI foundation model for automated cerebrovascular diagnosis with multicenter MRA database construction.
bullets: Integrates advanced deep learning to overcome subjectivity and inconsistency in traditional vascular analysis.

title: Fabrication and Testing of High-Performance Superconducting Joints of Magnesium Diboride
supervisors: Prof. Song Gao & Prof. Xinwei Cai
summary: First superconducting joints for multi-filament MgB₂ wires using Mg(BH₄)₂ precursor; device and process optimization for joint density.
bullets: Developed custom filling device and optimized joint density to enable next-generation MgB₂ MRI systems.

title: Postoperative Brain Function Mapping Using fMRI
institution: Peking University, The National Institute of Health Data Science
summary: First fMRI study validating cortical functional changes following Cuistow surgery.
bullets: Led patient recruitment, MRI scanning, and statistical analysis for actionable neuroimaging insights in postoperative assessment.

title: Super-Resolution T₁ Mapping from Multi-Orientation MRI Acquisitions
institution: Peking University, The National Institute of Health Data Science (with Boston Children's Hospital CRL)
summary: Accelerated reconstruction pipeline fusing orthogonal MRI acquisitions into isotropic high-resolution volumes with quantitative T₁ and PD maps.
bullets: Iterative estimation combining multi-orientation acquisitions for improved resolution.

title: Standing Balance, Cognitive Function, and Depression in Middle-Aged and Elderly Adults (China)
institution: Peking University, National School of Development, CHARLS Program
summary: Population-based analysis linking standing balance with cognitive and depressive status using three waves of CHARLS data.
bullets: Validated clinically meaningful associations for aging research and public health.

title: Enhanced Vestibular Disorder Diagnosis Using Machine Learning and Structural Data Integration (UPVD)
summary: Radiomics and machine learning on 3D-FLAIR/T1 vestibular MRI to improve UPVD diagnosis.
bullets: Extracted >1,500 features and implemented XGBoost classification for clinically actionable insights.
